title: Payments API
description: The Payments API enables merchants to initiate, check the status of, cancel, and void payments. The payment operations are performed for the orders created by the merchants. This ensures that each payment is associated with a specific order and has rich data associated with it.

apis.heading: Initiate a Payment
apis.description: Initiates a payment for a specific order. Use this API to initiate a payment for the order created. The default method for processing the initiated payment is via card. Additional payment methods can be enabled using the Payment Methods API.

reqParams.name: paymentMethod
reqParams.description: Specifies the method in which the payment is to be processed.

possibleValues.description: Use this payment method to make card transactions for the order.
Use this payment method to process card-not-present(CNP) refunds for the order.
Make payments using Klarna.
Use this payment method to make payments using tokenised card if available. Used during online payments.
Use this to make payments with gift cards/vouchers.

reqParams.name: orderId
reqParams.description: Unique identifier of order to initiate payment for.

reqParams.name: terminalId
reqParams.description: Unique identifier of terminal.

reqParams.name: amount
reqParams.description: Use this parameter to make partial payments on the order by sending in the amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g., 10 SEK is 1000 in amount). This is only required for purchase transactions.

reqParams.name: initialOrderReference
reqParams.description: initialOrderReference

reqParams.name: paymentMethodParams
reqParams.description: Method-specific parameters

reqParams.name: tokenId
reqParams.description: Token ID is mandatory if CTOKEN is passed in as the payment method.
reqParams.conditional:
Dependent on using
CTOKEN as the payment method

reqParams.name: bic
reqParams.description: The BIC (Bank Identifier Code) is an ISO 9362 standard that uniquely identifies a bank in cross-border transactions. It is mandatory for the ACC2ACC payment method.

reqParams.name: ip
reqParams.description: ip

reqParams.name: swishMode
reqParams.description: Swish mode (e.g., ECOM)

reqParams.name: payeePhoneNumber
reqParams.description: Mandatory phone number for SWISH payments.

reqParams.name: code
reqParams.description: International dialing code (Eg., 46 for Sweden without '+')

reqParams.name: number
reqParams.description: National subscriber number (digits only)

reqParams.name: giftCardId
reqParams.description: Gift card identifier

reqParams.name: invoice
reqParams.description: Invoice payment configuration

reqParams.name: invoiceDistribution
reqParams.description: Distribution channel (e.g., EMAIL)

reqParams.name: contractReference
reqParams.description: Contract reference identifier

reqParams.name: debtCollectionEnabled
reqParams.description: Enable debt collection

reqParams.name: dueDate
reqParams.description: Due date or relative duration (e.g., 30d)

reqParams.name: invoicePaymentMethods
reqParams.description: Specifies the payment methods available for settling an invoice.

possibleValues.description: Standard bank transfer initiated manually by the payer.
Automated account-to-account (A2A) transfer using open banking or similar mechanisms.
Payment made via credit or debit cards through a supported card network.

reqParams.name: reminder
reqParams.description: Enable reminders

reqParams.name: serialSplitPaymentsMode
reqParams.description: An array of amounts in smallest currency unit to split the payment. The order of the amounts in the array will determine the order of the split payments.

reqParams.name: refundProcessingParams
reqParams.description: Parameters for processing refunds.

reqParams.name: purchasePaymentId
reqParams.description: For SWISH, NSWISH, KLARNA and CARD_NP(card-not-present) partial refunds, it is mandatory to send in the Original Purchase Payment ID to which the refund will be initiated.

reqParams.name: refundReason
reqParams.description: Indicates the reason for the return request. Mandatory for CARD_NP refunds.

possibleValues.description: This type of refund refers to when a customer initiates a return of a product or service they purchased.
When there is a suspected system malfunction which demands a return request, this type is used.
When there is a suspected irregularity/fraud requiring a return request, this type is used.
This type of refund request is submitted when there are duplicate transactions noticed in the system.
The "OTHER" refund request type encompasses any refund request that do not fit into the specific categories mentioned above.

reqParams.name: otherReason
reqParams.description: Describe the reason for the return request that do not fall into any of the predefined categories. Mandatory when "OTHER" is selected as the `refundReason`

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request, indicated as either 'SUCCESS' or 'ERROR'.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: paymentId
resParams.description: A payment ID assigned to each payment instance. Ensure that this ID is stored securely and tied to an identifiable entity until the payment completion.

resParams.name: interAppJWT
resParams.description: This key can be used in the inter-app flow to be passed in as part of the app switch. This is an optional key that can improve inter-app transaction initiation performance. This is currently only functional for CARD payments.

resParams.name: paymentUrl
resParams.description: Direct URL for payment processing.

resParams.name: qr
resParams.description: QR code data for payment.

resParams.name: qrData
resParams.description: Raw QR code data for payment processing.

resParams.name: qrVisibleFor
resParams.description: Duration for which the QR code remains visible.

resParams.name: qrLink
resParams.description: Link to the QR code for payment.

resParams.name: klarnaClientToken
resParams.description: Client token for Klarna payment processing.

resParams.name: paymentToken
resParams.description: Token for payment processing.

resParams.name: sveaInitiationParams
resParams.description: Initiation parameters for Svea payment processing.

resParams.name: invoiceDetails
resParams.description: Invoice details for the payment when applicable.

resParams.name: invoiceId
resParams.description: Unique identifier for the invoice.

resParams.name: invoicePdfUrl
resParams.description: URL to download the invoice PDF.

resParams.name: iban
resParams.description: International Bank Account Number for the invoice.

resParams.name: accountHolderName
resParams.description: Name of the account holder for the invoice.

resParams.name: bic
resParams.description: Bank Identifier Code for the invoice.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.

apis.heading: Cancel a Payment
apis.description: Cancels the initiated payment. You can use this API to cancel the payment instance created. However, you cannot cancel a completed payment. If the payment is already completed, you can use the Void a Payment API to void the specific payment.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request, indicated as either 'SUCCESS' or 'ERROR'.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: paymentStatus
resParams.description: Describes the status of the payment. The possible values are:

possibleValues.description: The payment was completed and cannot be cancelled.
Cannot cancel a failed payment.
The payment attempt has been cancelled.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.

apis.heading: Void a Payment
apis.description: Voids a completed payment. If a merchant identifies an issue with a payment soon after it has been completed, use this API to promptly void the payment, thereby preventing potential issues arising from incorrect payments. Note that payment has to be in status ‘PAYMENT_COMPLETED’ and void triggered before 23:00 UTC. For orders with delayCapture set to true, void can be initiated anytime before the capture call.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request, indicated as either 'SUCCESS' or 'ERROR'.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: voidStatus
resParams.description: Depicts the status of the void command.

possibleValues.description: Void is initiated.
The payment cannot be voided either because the transaction is not completed or the void period has exceeded. Voids need to be initiated before 23:50 CET on the same date as the transaction.
Void is completed.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.

apis.heading: Capture Payment
apis.description: Use this API to complete the authorisation and capture a payment while using the pre-auth flow or to capture a payment when the delayCapture flag is set.

reqParams.name: amount
reqParams.description: This is only valid if the authMode was set to `PRE-AUTH`. This sets the final authorisation amount to the amount specified and initiates capture on the payment.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request, indicated as either 'SUCCESS' or 'ERROR'.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.

apis.heading: Check Capture Status
apis.description: Retrieves the status of the capture payment request. Capture payments for delayed capture is an asynchronous request that is processed in batches. Once the initial capturePayments request is made, please make this call after approximately 30 minutes to get the response

reqParams.name: paymentId
reqParams.description: Input the payment ID received during initiate payment request in the URL.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request, indicated as either 'SUCCESS' or 'ERROR'.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: captureStatus
resParams.description: Denotes the status of the capture request for payment.

possibleValues.description: Payment yet to be captured.
Payment captured successfully.
Error encountered while processing capture request.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request.
